claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17, this is an important age to consider for Child Benefit eligibility. Let me ask you:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- Scottish Highers
- T-levels
- Home education (if started before they turned 16)
- Certain training courses (up to 20 hours per week)
- NVQs up to level 3

Please let me know if Alex is enrolled in any approved education or training.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any approved education or training at the moment. They finished school but aren't doing A-levels or anything like that right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information for determining your eligibility.

Let me ask you a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you (or your partner, if you have one) responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for their upbringing and care?

Also, just to confirm: **Are you or your partner currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, or is this a new inquiry?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for their care and upbringing. This is a new inquiry - I'm not currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, I'm just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim it.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming those details. Let me ask one more important question:

**When did Alex turn 16?** I need to know the specific date (or at least the month and year) when Alex reached their 16th birthday.

This is crucial because the rules around Child Benefit eligibility change significantly at age 16, and there are specific time limits that apply depending on whether a child continues in approved education or training after that age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 16 in September last year - so that would be September 2023.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me explain the situation based on the UK Child Benefit rules:

**Child Benefit eligibility ends when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training.** When a child stays in approved education or training, Child Benefit can continue until they turn 20 (or until they leave education/training, whichever comes first).

In Alex's case:
- Alex turned 16 in September 2023
- Alex is now 17 years old
- Alex is **not** currently in approved education or training

Since Alex is over 16 and is not in approved education or training, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The eligibility for Child Benefit would have ended when Alex turned 16 (in September 2023), as they did not continue in approved education or training after that point.

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again, but only if they do so before turning 20 years old.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, having turned 16 in September 2023. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility for a child ends when they turn 16 unless they continue in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any approved education or training. Since Alex is over 16 and not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 (up until age 20) if the child remains in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex, who is 17 years old. Alex turned 16 in September 2023 and is not currently in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility ends at age 16 unless the child continues in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 (up until age 20) if the child remains in approved education or training.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility ends at age 16 unless the child continues in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
